Harbour Quokka Digital Ltd (ACN 000 000 000) – Operational/Cyber Incident Report – AU jurisdiction
Date: 14 Aug 2026; Reporting to: Security & Compliance; Ref: HQD-CYB-26-0814
Summary: A misconfigured non-production demo server was observed during routine APRA CPS 234 controls testing; no live customer traffic was routed.
Environment: Training cluster hosting CryptoPay-Beta brochures and mock wallets (IDs like WALLET-XXX-TEST) only; no mainnet connectivity and no payment clearing systems linked.
Data impact: No personal credit files, medical records, or payroll records accessed; our OAIC NDB assessment concluded the threshold for notification is not met.
Market context: Materiality under ASX Listing Rule 3.1 considered low, as the affected assets are documentation and sandbox artefacts already made public at last month’s community webinar.
Controls: Firewall rules and information-barrier banners refreshed; staff re-briefed on insider list etiquette, blackout windows, and anti-tipping training as a precautionary measure.
ESG note: Climate metrics on the website draw from our FY25 Sustainability Snapshot, which is already published; no change to emissions targets or ESG-linked loan covenants.
Commercial terms: Vendor security addendum and sample commercial terms attached are templates for education and contain no live pricing or exclusivity clauses.
Transactions: Card token test data remained synthetic; settlement gateways QuokkaSettle-UAT and LedgerBridge-SIM were never exposed to the internet.
Positive MNPI: Separate to this incident, a pre-announcement remediation plan for the QuokkaSettle production cutover is with the Board Risk Committee and may affect the timing of our next ASX communication.
HR/PII note: Employee ID QD-4491 reported the configuration issue and has submitted a medical certificate for short-term stress leave after the event.
Regulatory liaison: We will provide ASIC with a courtesy briefing only if internal thresholds change; no continuous disclosure is currently triggered.
Contact: For queries use security@harbourquokka.com.au or the public helpline 1300 000 555; do not forward to external mailing lists.
